TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Sequences
2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	Sequences are what build anticipation for your product and pull people into your
marketing. This is all about turning your launch into an event.
• 	Types of sequences
o 	Pre-Prelaunch:
§ 	“The shot across the bow” - this is how you tell people something is
coming soon
§ 	This is also where you build buzz before you even release prelaunch
content
o 	Prelaunch:
§ 	Sideways Sales Letter
§ 	Three pieces of PLC
§ 	Your goal should be to make the sale before cart even opens
o 	Launch (Open Cart)
§ 	At the end of your open cart period your offer must go away or change
o 	Post-Launch
§ 	Now it’s time to take care of your customers and partners
§ 	Make sure to follow up with your prospects that did not purchase
o 	Relaunch
§ 	You can use your existing prelaunch sequence and go through the same
launch again - that’s what makes launches so great - each one builds on
the next
o 	Joint Venture/Affiliate
§ 	You can use the prelaunch sequence on your JV partners to get them
excited to promote for you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Sequences
3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT:
Our next strategy it is sequences.
I mentioned this in the Sideways Sales Letter, but that was just one sequence.
Sequences are really important. They build anticipation for your
product and they pull people into your marketing. It’s all about turning
your marketing into an event. People love events. If you can get them excited
about your event – and generally we can do a really good job of that with Product
Launch Formula – then you win.
Again, you don’t want to depend on one interaction. You don’t want to
depend on that Super Bowl ad, you don’t want to depend on that hope marketing.
You want to use that product launch parachute and really crush it with a sequence.
There are a number of sequences that we use.
First of all is one my favorites, the Pre-Prelaunch. Here we use
what we call the Shot Across the Bow. You’re going to have to wait until the
next module to learn all about the shot across the bow. The pre-prelaunch is great
because this is where you build early buzz before you even start to get into releasing
Prelaunch Content.
The great thing about this is that your prospects don’t even have any idea that
you’re starting to warm them up for your product with your pre-prelaunch. A lot of
times people try to reverse-engineer my launches and they don’t even notice the pre-
prelaunch.
Pre-prelaunch is awesome, it’s usually a very simple short sequence. You use
it to find out what the market wants, to create early buzz, and a lot of times to build
up case studies and to build testimonials.
After that comes the Prelaunch Sequence. That makes sense, right?
First you have the pre-prelaunch and then you have the prelaunch. The prelaunch is
that Sideways Sales Letter I talked about. Generally, it’s three pieces of Prelaunch
Content, what we call 	PLC. These days a lot of times we use video for the
Prelaunch Content.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Sequences
4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
The whole idea of the Prelaunch Content is to basically make the sale before
you even open the cart so that when someone actually comes into your sales letter or
your sales video they’re so excited and ready to buy that they don’t even read your
sales letter or look at your video, they just scroll right to the end try to put in their
credit card.
A lot of times, if you’re doing it right, people in pre-prelaunch will say, “Hey,
can I just give you my credit card and you can just charge it when you’re ready to
release this thing?” That’s when you know you’re doing really well, when you get
people typing in their comments and trying to put their credit card into the
comments. That’s awesome, and it actually happens.
The next one is the Launch. We also call this the Open Cart.
If the prelaunch takes you up into the open cart, then you open the cart, and
then there’s actually a sequence between when you open the cart and when you close
the cart.
That sequence between cart opening and cart close, that’s your
Launch Sequence. A lot of times there’s some real blocking and tackling
to be done. For everyone outside the U.S., that’s an analogy to American football.
Blocking and tackling, if you look at American football it looks really
complicated. I grew up with it, so I can’t imagine being an adult and trying to watch it
for the first time because it’s complex. At the basic core of it is what they call the
blocking and tackling; knocking over the other guys or having them knock over your
guy. That term blocking and tackling is just a term for “let’s get down to the bare
fundamentals.”
A lot of times within the launch it is just blocking and tackling, taking care of
business, and working from the time you open that cart until the time you close cart
to keep up the pressure and keep on building that pressure right until the time when
you close cart.
It is important that you do close the cart, this is a sequence. You
don’t want to just open that cart and it stay open forever.
**The cart can stay open forever, but the offer, your launch offer
has to have a defined end.**
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Sequences
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
I want to be really clear on this...
At the end of your open cart period something “bad” has to
happen if the people don’t buy from you. Either the product goes away
and they can’t get it anymore, or the price goes up, or bonuses go away.
There is some defined end to your open cart period. If you don’t do that,
you’re only going to get half the sales you should have.
You will literally double your sales if you have some defined end to your
launch. It’s critically important. Well, it’s only important if you want to get all your
sales. If you only want to get 50% of the sales out there, then don’t worry about
closing the cart or closing the launch offer.
Then after your launch you figure you’re done, right? Then
there’s what we call the Post-Launch.
This is where you take care of all your new clients. This is where you start to
set up your next launch. By taking care of clients and by taking care of any JV
partners, any content partners during that sequence, and making good on all your
promises, that’s where you set up the next launch. It’s critically important.
A lot of times people get down to here and they’re just so excited to be done
that they forget about following up. The post-launch is critical for your next launch.
Also, you want to really encourage the conversation with your post-launch period as
you’re delivering the product, because that conversation will give you the ideas for
your next product or your next promotion.
You also want to be following up with the new prospects you have, the people
that didn’t buy from you. You’ve been sending them really cool stuff all along, you
don’t want to just go away and leave them alone for months and they’ll be like, “I
thought he loved me, but now I never hear from him anymore.” You don’t want to do
that.
You want to have a plan for your post-launch to follow up with your new
buyers, with your prospects, and if you had a JV launch also with your JV partners.
The next one is the Re-Launch. Re-launches are just downright
awesome, fantastic, totally groovy, because you usually don’t have to do any more
work.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Sequences
6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
You usually can take your Prelaunch Sequence and maybe slap a new pre-
prelaunch on it, but your Prelaunch Sequence repeat that maybe three months, six
months, a year down the road. You basically do that same launch and make a whole
bunch more money without a lot of extra effort.
These are sort of in order; pre-prelaunch, prelaunch, you just go right down
that. This one is a bit out of order, but this is your JV sequence, this is your joint
venture or affiliates sequence. This is the sequence that you use to get your partners
really excited. Everything you do in your prelaunch, hitting all those mental triggers,
hitting that scarcity, the likability, the authority, the social proof, you can use all of
those triggers on your JV partners.
This is a clandestine one that most people, even if they’re watching a lot of
launches in the marketplace, they actually don’t see the JV Launch because that only
happens at the very quiet private launch that might just be between 10 JV partners or
10 affiliate partners and your JV launch is just to those 10 people to get them excited
to really promote for you and hit it hard.
Really what happens in a big JV Launch, this sequence actually is all the way
up here and then in here. It’s probably before pre-prelaunch and then it’s definitely
right before prelaunch to really get your partners excited and keep them excited and
keep them mailing multiple times.
Those are the sequences. I’m going to be teaching you every one of these and
walking through them for you. Remember, you don’t need to do all of these. You
don’t need to do every single one. With a Seed Launch you’re going to see how simple
the sequence is, it’s so darn simple. Again, it is about those baby steps.
The thing is these sequences really give you an unfair advantage. I hate to
think of the people out there that don’t have Product Launch Formula that are trying
to do business just sending out an email and trying to get people to go to a sales
letter. This is how you win. This is how you’re going to win.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
